pr.title: Match and improve several source functions
pr.author: itsgrimetime

summary: Open PR batching several independent decomp matching/source-shaping improvements. The decomp.dev report recorded +844 matched bytes overall, with new 100% matches for `hsd_80398C04` in baselib particle code and `__THPRestartDefinition` in Dolphin THP, plus near-match improvements for Ice Mountain, menu count, and fighter item handling. Review focused on reusing the existing `GET_GROUND()` convention instead of a local user-data helper; the author fixed that and received approval, though the slice shows the PR still open.

key_files.reason: Matched `__THPRestartDefinition` by adding a narrow `THPRestartFields` overlay for `nMCU`, `currMCU`, and `RST`.
Matched `hsd_80398C04` by using the named `hsd_804D0908` slot array and correcting initialized particle fields.
Improved `grIceMt_801F9ACC` by changing return shape, ID types, stack/local layout, and ground-data access style.
Updated the `grIceMt_801F9ACC` prototype from `void` to `int` to match the implementation.
Improved `ft_800895E0` by removing a temporary `Item_GObj*` and reusing `fp->item_gobj` directly.
Improved `mnCount_8025092C` by reshaping the comparison and `CountEntry tmp` lifetime.

what_changed: The PR body described a batch of independent source matching improvements for `grIceMt_801F9ACC`, `hsd_80398C04`, `__THPRestartDefinition`, `ft_800895E0`, and `mnCount_8025092C`.
The decomp.dev bot reported matched code at 70.29%, +0.02% and +844 bytes for GALE01.

`hsd_80398C04` moved from 99.64% to 100.00%; the code now uses `&hsd_804D0908[linkNo]` instead of offsetting from `hsd_804D08E8`, removes the old near-match TODO, initializes `primCol` to 0xFF, initializes `envCol` to 0, and writes `rotateTarget` instead of `sizeTarget`.
`__THPRestartDefinition` moved from 99.76% to 100.00%; the function casts `THPFileInfo*` to a local `THPRestartFields*` overlay before setting `RST`, `nMCU`, and `currMCU`.

`grIceMt_801F9ACC` improved from 96.34% to 98.90%; it now returns `int did`, early-returns 0, returns `did` at the end, changes `id` to `s32`, adds `PAD_STACK(16)`, introduces an `HSD_JObj** new_var`, and replaces direct `user_data` access with `GET_GROUND()` calls.
`gricemt.h` was kept in sync by changing the `grIceMt_801F9ACC` declaration from `void` to `int`.
`mnCount_8025092C` improved from 99.38% to 99.92%; it hoists `CountEntry tmp`, removes `PAD_STACK(8)`, and rewrites the row-selection comparison as `entries[min].val < entries[j].val`.
`ft_800895E0` improved from 98.86% to 99.98%; it removes a temporary `Item_GObj* temp` and checks/calls through `fp->item_gobj` directly.
Verification listed in the PR body was `python configure.py --require-protos`, `ninja build/GALE01/report.json`, and `ninja`.
smart_moves: Used an exact named global, `hsd_804D0908`, to resolve a particle relocation-style near-match rather than preserving pointer arithmetic from `hsd_804D08E8`.
Applied a very small THP overlay struct instead of broadly redefining `THPFileInfo`, keeping the offset-sensitive restart fields localized to `__THPRestartDefinition`.
Changed `grIceMt_801F9ACC` to return the existing `did` status, which makes the control-flow and ABI shape explicit while preserving the function's internal flagging behavior.
Synchronized the Ice Mountain header prototype with the implementation immediately, avoiding `--require-protos` drift.
Responded to review by removing a local user-data helper and reusing `GET_GROUND()`, matching project convention and reducing duplicate access patterns.

Used small source-shape adjustments, such as direct `fp->item_gobj` loads, hoisted `CountEntry tmp`, added/removed `PAD_STACK`, and a temporary `new_var`, to improve match percentages without large semantic rewrites.
decomp_lessons: Prefer existing project access macros such as `GET_GROUND(gobj)` over ad hoc helpers or direct `gobj->user_data` access when the surrounding subsystem already has a convention.
When asm bytes are close but relocations differ, audit whether the source should reference the exact global symbol rather than computing an address from a neighboring global.
For external SDK code, a narrow overlay struct can be a useful matching tactic for fields at known offsets, but it should stay localized and evidence-based.
Return-type corrections are part of matching work: if a function naturally tracks a status like `did`, changing `void` to `int` and updating early returns/prototypes may be the right shape.

Field-name accuracy matters for both semantics and matching; near-matches can expose that initialization belongs to `primCol`, `envCol`, or `rotateTarget` rather than similarly typed neighboring fields.
Small local-variable lifetime changes can be enough to move a function from near-match to match; hoisting a temp or removing an unnecessary temp can alter stack/register allocation.
naming_conventions: `GET_GROUND()` is the preferred existing convention for retrieving `Ground*` from a ground GObj in this area.
Stage functions in this slice retain address-style names such as `grIceMt_801F9ACC`, `grIceMt_801F993C`, and `grIceMt_801FA854`.
Header declarations in `gricemt.h` keep address comments like `/* 1F9ACC */` aligned with the function symbol.
Dolphin THP internals preserve SDK-style names and capitalization, including `__THPRestartDefinition`, `THPRestartFields`, `nMCU`, `currMCU`, and `RST`.
Baselib globals retain address-derived names such as `hsd_804D0908`; when available, use the exact global rather than pointer arithmetic through another address global.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use a cast to a small padding overlay struct to force accesses to the desired offsets in `THPFileInfo`-backed data.
Replace pointer arithmetic from an adjacent global with a direct named global-array reference to fix relocation-sensitive matches.
Tune stack/register shape with local declaration placement: hoist `CountEntry tmp`, add `PAD_STACK(16)` in `grIceMt_801F9ACC`, and remove obsolete `PAD_STACK(8)` in `mnCount_8025092C`.
Remove temporary object-pointer locals when the target code reloads a struct field directly, as in `ft_800895E0` using `fp->item_gobj`.
Introduce a temporary pointer such as `new_var = &gp->gv.icemt2.xC8` before assignment to steer generated code shape.
Change comparison operand order in selection/insertion logic when the decompiled control-flow shape is otherwise close.
Update function return type and all return sites to model a status value in `r3` rather than leaving the function `void`.

review_feedback.feedback: Asked whether the new Ice Mountain user-data helper could use existing `GET_GROUND()`.
Author replied they would check, then reported the latest push removed the local `user_data` helper and switched `grIceMt_801F9ACC` call sites to `GET_GROUND()`.
Review flow shows an initial `CHANGES_REQUESTED` review followed by `APPROVED` after the fix.

searchable_terms: Match and improve several source functions
